Title: Reciprocal Regulation of Protein Synthesis and Carbon Metabolism for Thylakoid Membrane Biogenesis

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: HSP70/RBCS2 constitutive promoter (for GFP construct in applied strain UVM4); NA for RNAi knockdown of endogenous gene
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Chloroplast thylakoid membrane biogenesis region (T-zone/CTM) for D1-related output; chloroplast for acetyl-CoA supply
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein detected under photoautotrophic, mixotrophic, and heterotrophic conditions; RNA-binding/RNP form specifically observed under mixotrophic conditions (light plus acetate)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein purification plus mass spectrometry; RNAi knockdown; cpPDC enzyme assay; UV cross-linking/filter binding RNA assay; SEC with RNase sensitivity; RNA co-immunoprecipitation; FISH/IF localization; subcellular fractionation; GFP targeting
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively accumulated under mixotrophic, heterotrophic, and photoautotrophic conditions at protein level
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: CC-406; UVM4; WT-NE recipient background used for transgenics
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong DLA2 knockdown caused severe growth retardation under mixotrophic conditions; reduced cpPDC activity to 15%-25% of WT; slight reduction of PSII under mixotrophic/heterotrophic conditions; pleiotropic effects under photoautotrophy
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: CC-406; UVM4; WT-NE; RNAi lines iDLA2-1, iDLA2-2, iDLA2-3; mutants FuD7 and nac2-26 for comparative analyses
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast; both stroma and thylakoid-associated; also associated with chloroplast translation membrane fractions/T-zone under mixotrophic conditions
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light versus dark; acetate availability; mixotrophic/photoautotrophic/heterotrophic growth; DCMU treatment; addition of psbA 5'UTR RNA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RBP63 was identified as DLA2, the chloroplast pyruvate dehydrogenase E2 subunit. 2. DLA2 is dual-functional: it supports cpPDC activity for acetyl-CoA production and also binds psbA mRNA in a light- and acetate-dependent RNP complex. 3. DLA2 knockdown reduces cpPDC activity and alters D1 synthesis, especially under mixotrophic conditions. 4. DLA2 is required for efficient localization of psbA mRNA to the chloroplast T-zone/CTM, linking carbon metabolism with thylakoid membrane protein synthesis. 5. psbA RNA reciprocally inhibits cpPDC activity, indicating bidirectional regulation.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dihydrolipoamide acetyltransferase (pyruvate dehydrogenase E2 subunit); contains lipoyl attachment domain, E3-binding region, and 2-oxoacid dehydrogenase catalytic domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chloroplast acetyl-CoA synthesis via cpPDC and promotes psbA mRNA localization/D1 synthesis needed for PSII-thylakoid membrane biogenesis under mixotrophic conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats DLA2 primarily as a dual-function metabolic enzyme/RNA-binding factor rather than a classical pathway-only enzyme; active cpPDC is likely the >2.8 MDa complex, while the ~700 kDa mixotrophic complex is an RNP; recombinant DLA2 binds RNA intrinsically but lacks psbA specificity in vitro, implying specificity is conferred by additional factors in vivo
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acetyl-CoA supplies fatty acid/lipid synthesis for thylakoid membranes; D1 protein is essential for photosystem II assembly and photosynthetic membrane biogenesis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Mass spectrometry peptide identification; in silico genome/TargetP/RNABindR analyses; no public omics accession reported
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: psbA mRNA; cpPDC subunits E1 and E3 (complex context); RBP40 (co-fractionation marker, not direct binding shown)
